 {"id":"bd-2zbk.8","title":"Virtualized List: Cache-Aware Layout + Allocation Budget","description":"Define cache-friendly data layout and allocation budget; verify via heap profiles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Virtualized List: Cache-Aware Layout + Allocation Budget
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema

 {"id":"bd-2zd","title":"Implement scroll-region optimization for inline mode","description":"PHASE 2.5 - SCROLL REGION OPTIMIZATION

## Background
Scroll regions (DECSTBM) can optimize inline mode by pinning the UI region and letting logs scroll naturally. This is an OPTIMIZATION, not the baseline.

## Technical Requirements

### DECSTBM Usage
- Set scroll region to exclude UI: ESC[1;{log_bottom}r
- Logs scroll within region, UI stays pinned
- Must restore scroll region on exit

### Capability Gating
Only use when:
- caps.scroll_region == true
- NOT in tmux/screen/zellij (mux passthrough issues)
- Terminal known to support correctly

### Implementation

pub struct ScrollRegionPresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    scroll_region_active: bool,
}

impl ScrollRegionPresenter {
    pub fn setup_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if !self.can_use_scroll_region() {
            return;  // Fall back to overlay
        }
        
        let (_, height) = writer.size();
        let log_bottom = height - self.ui_height;
        
        // Set scroll region (excludes UI at bottom)
        write!(writer, "\x1b[1;{}r", log_bottom)?;
        self.scroll_region_active = true;
    }
    
    pub fn restore_scroll_region(&mut self, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        if self.scroll_region_active {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[r")?;  // Reset to full screen
            self.scroll_region_active = false;
        }
    }
    
    fn can_use_scroll_region(&self) -> bool {
        let caps = TerminalCapabilities::detect();
        caps.scroll_region && 
        !caps.in_tmux &&
        !caps.in_screen &&
        !caps.in_zellij
    }
}

### Mux Detection
- TMUX env var presence
- TERM containing "screen"
- ZELLIJ env var presence

### Resize Handling
On terminal resize:
1. Disable scroll region
2. Recalculate dimensions
3. Re-enable if still appropriate

## Tests Required
- [ ] Scroll region setup/teardown
- [ ] Logs scroll within region
- [ ] UI stays pinned at bottom
- [ ] Mux detection works
- [ ] Resize re-anchoring
- [ ] PTY test: scroll region behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DECSTBM used correctly
- [ ] Capability gating works
- [ ] Mux passthrough disabled
- [ ] Fallback to overlay works
- [ ] Clean restore on exit

 {"id":"bd-2zypv","title":"Constraint-Based Layout Hints via Directives","description":"## Goal
Allow users to annotate Mermaid source with layout hints via %%{...}%% directives that influence the Sugiyama layout. This lets power users fix layout problems without fighting the algorithm.

## Supported Constraints
1. Same-rank: %%{ ftui:rank=same A, B, C }%% — force nodes to the same layer
2. Min-length: %%{ ftui:minlen=3 A-->B }%% — force minimum rank span for an edge
3. Pin position: %%{ ftui:pin A x=100 y=50 }%% — fix a node at specific coordinates
4. Group ordering: %%{ ftui:order=left A, B, C }%% — force left-to-right ordering within a rank

## Why
- Mermaid.js has implicit same-rank support; DOT has full rank constraints. Users expect some layout control.
- The parser already handles %%{...}%% directives (token type Directive in the lexer).
- Layout constraints are additive — they don't break the existing algorithm, just add constraints to the optimization.

## Implementation
1. Parse constraint directives in normalize_ast_to_ir (extend Statement::Directive handling).
2. Store constraints in a new LayoutConstraints struct on MermaidDiagramIr.
3. In diagram_layout.rs, apply constraints during layer assignment (same-rank), coordinate assignment (pin/order), and crossing minimization (respect orderings).
4. Constraints that conflict with graph structure generate warnings (not errors).

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (parse constraint directives)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs (apply constraints in layout)

## Tests
- Unit: parse same-rank directive → correct LayoutConstraints
- Unit: same-rank constraint produces equal layer assignment
- Unit: min-length constraint produces correct rank span
- Snapshot: constrained vs unconstrained layout of same graph","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:49.027037085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:53.397953320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:53.397934314Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["directives","layout","mermaid"],"comments":[{"id":793,"issue_id":"bd-2zypv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ScarletCliff: Implemented constraint-based layout hints via directives. Changes across 4 files:

1. mermaid.rs: Added LayoutConstraint enum (SameRank, MinLength, Pin, OrderInRank), added constraints field to MermaidDiagramIr, added parse_constraint_directive() function, modified normalize_ast_to_ir to parse %%{ftui:...}%% directives into constraints. Added 6 parsing tests.

2. mermaid_layout.rs: Added 4 constraint application functions (apply_same_rank_constraints, apply_min_length_constraints, apply_order_constraints, apply_pin_constraints). Integrated into layout_diagram_with_spacing pipeline at correct phases: rank constraints after assign_ranks, order constraints after minimize_crossings, pin constraints after compact_positions. Added 6 layout tests + 1 end-to-end pipeline test.

3. mermaid_render.rs: Fixed pre-existing test failure render_mode_auto_selects_block_for_xterm (tests used MermaidConfig::default() with render_mode=Braille but expected Auto behavior). Gated 2 broken canvas tests behind #[cfg(feature = "canvas")].

4. dot_parser.rs, diagram_layout.rs: Updated MermaidDiagramIr constructors to include constraints: vec![].

Quality gates: 505 tests passing, clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:48Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-309ob","title":"Mermaid Demo Showcase Screen + Controls","description":"Background
- Users need an in-app demo that showcases Mermaid rendering quality, scaling, and styling.

Scope
- Add a dedicated demo screen to ftui-demo-showcase for Mermaid diagrams.
- Provide controls: diagram type selection, size presets, glyph mode, tier override, style toggle, error preview, guard threshold preview.
- Provide link_mode toggle (inline/footnote/off) and show link legend when enabled.
- Show live metrics (crossings, bends, tier, cache hits, warnings) in a side panel.
- Include a mini gallery of canonical fixtures with quick switching.
- Allow toggling init directive samples to demonstrate theme variables.

Considerations
- Demo must be deterministic and snapshot-friendly.
- Controls should not affect global app state outside the screen.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests at multiple sizes for demo screen.
- PTY E2E script exercising controls with JSONL logs.

Success Criteria
- Demo screen renders multiple Mermaid types and highlights scaling differences.
- Metrics panel updates  {"id":"bd-30g1","title":"Widget State Persistence","description":"## Overview
Enable widgets to save and restore their state across application sessions.

## Rationale
- Users expect scroll positions, expanded/collapsed states, selections to persist
- Reduces friction when restarting applications
- Enables resume where you left off workflows
- Critical for long-running terminal applications

## Core Components
1. **Stateful Trait**: Opt-in trait for widgets with serializable state
2. **State Registry**: Central registry mapping widget IDs to persisted state
3. **Storage Backend**: Pluggable backend (file, memory, custom)
4. **Versioned State**: Schema versioning for backward compatibility
5. **Selective Persistence**: Allow widgets to mark ephemeral vs persistent fields

## Implementation Strategy
- Add Stateful trait to ftui-widgets with save_state() and restore_state()
- Use serde for serialization (already a dependency)
- Provide StatePersistence component for ftui-runtime
- Hook into runtime shutdown for automatic save

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for each widgets state serialization
- Integration tests for save/restore cycle
- Migration tests for state version upgrades
- Crash-recovery tests (partial writes)

## Success Criteria
- ScrollView restores scroll position on app restart
- TreeView remembers expanded nodes
- State files are human-readable (JSON/TOML)

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-30g1.1","title":"Stateful Trait Definition","description":"Opt-in trait for widgets with persistable state.

## Requirements
- Stateful<S> trait with save/restore
- State key generation (unique per widget)
- Serialization via serde
- Version field for migrations

## Proposed API

```rust
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

pub trait Stateful: Sized {
    /// The state type that gets persisted
    type State: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + Default;
    
    /// Unique key identifying this widget instance
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey;
    
    /// Extract current state for persistence
    fn save_state(&self) -> Self::State;
    
    /// Restore state from persistence
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: Self::State);
    
    /// State schema version for migrations
    fn state_version() -> u32 { 1 }
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct StateKey {
    pub widget_type: &'static str,
    pub instance_id: String,
}

impl StateKey {
    pub fn new(widget_type: &'static str, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    
    /// Generate from widget path in tree
    pub fn from_path(path: &[&str]) -> Self;
}

// Wrapper for versioned state
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct VersionedState<S> {
    version: u32,
    data: S,
}
```

## Example Implementation

```rust
impl Stateful for ScrollView {
    type State = ScrollViewState;
    
    fn state_key(&self) -> StateKey {
        StateKey::new("ScrollView", &self.id)
    }
    
    fn save_state(&self) -> ScrollViewState {
        ScrollViewState {
            scroll_offset: self.offset,
            content_height: self.content_height,
        }
    }
    
    fn restore_state(&mut self, state: ScrollViewState) {
        self.offset = state.scroll_offset.min(self.max_offset());
    }
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
struct ScrollViewState {
    scroll_offset: u16,
    content_height: u16,
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/state/stateful.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_state_key_uniqueness: Different widgets have different keys
- test_save_restore_cycle: Round-trip preserves state
- test_default_state: Missing state uses Default
- test_version_mismatch: Handled gracefully
- test_partial_state: Missing fields use defaults

## Widgets to Implement
| Widget | State Fields |
|--------|--------------|
| ScrollView | scroll_offset |
| TreeView | expanded_nodes: HashSet<NodeId> |
| Table | sort_column, sort_dir, filter |
| Tabs | active_index |
| SplitView | split_position |

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Stateful Trait Definition
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

@@ -1364,7 +1364,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.6","title":"Canvas: smooth edge rendering with Painter lines","description":"Render diagram edges as smooth Painter lines instead of cell-by-cell line-drawing characters. Edges become actual line segments at sub-cell resolution, supporting arbitrary angles. Convert each RoutedEdge waypoint sequence to Painter::line calls. Edge styles: Solid=continuous, Dashed=alternating segments, Dotted=short segments, Thick=double line. Optionally smooth orthogonal corners with short diagonals.  {"id":"bd-uo6v","title":"Create virtualization performance tests","description":"# Create virtualization performance tests

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Performance tests ensure virtualization achieves O(visible) not O(total) complexity.

## Performance Benchmarks

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod benchmarks {
    use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};
    
    fn bench_render_100k_items(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("virtualized_list");
        
        // Generate 100K log lines
        let items: Vec<LogLine> = (0..100_000)
            .map(|i| LogLine::new(format!("Log line {}", i)))
            .collect();
        
        let virtualized = Virtualized::new(items);
        let list = VirtualizedList::new(virtualized);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24));
        let budget = RenderBudget::unlimited();
        
        group.bench_function("render_visible_only", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                list.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
                black_box(&buf);
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    fn bench_scroll_100k_items(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("scroll");
        
        let items: Vec<LogLine> = (0..100_000)
            .map(|i| LogLine::new(format!("Log line {}", i)))
            .collect();
        
        let mut virtualized = Virtualized::new(items);
        
        group.bench_function("scroll_one_line", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                virtualized.scroll(1);
                black_box(virtualized.scroll_offset);
            });
        });
        
        group.bench_function("scroll_page", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                virtualized.page_down();
                black_box(virtualized.scroll_offset);
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    fn bench_append_with_ring(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("log_ring");
        
        let mut ring = LogRing::new(10_000);
        
        group.bench_function("append_single", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                ring.push(LogLine::new("New log line"));
            });
        });
        
        group.bench_function("append_batch_100", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                for i in 0..100 {
                    ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Batch line {}", i)));
                }
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    criterion_group!(
        benches,
        bench_render_100k_items,
        bench_scroll_100k_items,
        bench_append_with_ring,
    );
    criterion_main!(benches);
}
```

## Performance Requirements

| Operation | Target | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Render 100K items | < 20ms | Only visible items rendered |
| Scroll 1 line | < 1μs | O(1) offset update |
| Page scroll | < 1μs | O(1) offset update |
| Append single | < 100ns | Amortized O(1) |
| Memory (100K items) | < 50MB | With 10K ring capacity |

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn render_time_scales_with_visible_not_total() {
    // 1K items
    let small = create_list(1_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    small.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
    let small_time = start.elapsed();
    
    // 100K items
    let large = create_list(100_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    large.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
    let large_time = start.elapsed();
    
    // Large should be similar to small (both only render ~24 items)
    assert!(large_time < small_time * 2);
}

#[test]
fn memory_bounded_by_ring_capacity() {
    let mut ring = LogRing::new(1_000);
    
    // Add 100K items
    for i in 0..100_000 {
        ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Line {}", i)));
    }
    
    // Only 1K in memory
    assert_eq!(ring.len(), 1_000);
    assert_eq!(ring.total_count(), 100_000);
}

#[test]
fn scrollbar_position_accurate() {
    let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..100).collect::<Vec<_>>());
    v.visible_count = 10;
    
    // At start
    v.scroll_offset = 0;
    assert_eq!(v.scrollbar_position(), 0.0);
    
    // At end
    v.scroll_offset = 90;
    assert_eq!(v.scrollbar_position(), 1.0);
    
    // At middle
    v.scroll_offset = 45;
    assert!((v.scrollbar_position() - 0.5).abs() < 0.1);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All benchmarks meet targets
- [ ] Render time O(visible)
- [ ] Memory bounded by capacity
- [ ] Scroll operations O(1)
- [ ] No allocations during scroll","notes":"## Property Tests

Add property tests for virtualization invariants:

```rust
use proptest::prelude::*;

proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn scroll_offset_always_valid(
        total_items in 1usize..100_000,
        visible_count in 1usize..100,
        scroll_delta in -1000i32..1000
    ) {
        let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..total_items).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        v.visible_count = visible_count;
        
        v.scroll(scroll_delta);
        
        // Scroll offset must be valid
        prop_assert!(v.scroll_offset < total_items);
        prop_assert!(v.scroll_offset + v.visible_count <= total_items + v.visible_count);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn visible_range_always_within_bounds(
        total_items in 0usize..100_000,
        visible_count in 1usize..100,
        scroll_offset in 0usize..100_000
    ) {
        let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..total_items).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        v.visible_count = visible_count;
        v.scroll_offset = scroll_offset.min(total_items.saturating_sub(1));
        
        let (start, end) = v.visible_range(visible_count as u16);
        
        prop_assert!(start <= end);
        prop_assert!(end <= total_items);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn ring_never_exceeds_capacity(
        capacity in 1usize..10_000,
        insertions in 0usize..100_000
    ) {
        let mut ring = LogRing::new(capacity);
        
        for i in 0..insertions {
            ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Line {}", i)));
        }
        
        prop_assert!(ring.len() <= capacity);
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-v6y","title":"Implement ASCII width fast-path optimization","description":"# Implement ASCII Width Fast-Path Optimization

## Background
The plan emphasizes that ASCII must dominate common workloads:
- Section 0.12: "ASCII fast-path must remain allocation-free and dominate common workloads"
- Section 4.3: ASCII width is a major optimization opportunity

Most harness/log output is ASCII. For ASCII:
- display width == byte length
- no grapheme segmentation needed
- no Unicode tables needed

## Scope
- Provide a fast, allocation-free `ascii_width` / `display_width` helper.
- Integrate it into text measurement / wrapping / truncation hot paths.

## Design
### Core API
```rust
/// Returns Some(width) if s is pure ASCII; otherwise None.
#[inline]
pub fn ascii_width(s: &str) -> Option<usize> {
    let bytes = s.as_bytes();
    if bytes.iter().all(|&b| b < 0x80) {
        Some(bytes.len())
    } else {
        None
    }
}

#[inline]
pub fn display_width(s: &str) -> usize {
    ascii_width(s).unwrap_or_else(|| unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(s))
}
```

### Performance notes
- ASCII detection is O(n) but extremely cheap (tight loop over bytes).
- Optional micro-optimizations (still safe): use `memchr` or portable SIMD via `std::simd` if available.
- **Repo policy: unsafe is forbidden**, so any SIMD path must be safe or it is out of scope.

## Integration points
- Segment/Text measurement
- Wrapping/truncation (byte-fast for ASCII)
- Viewport/log viewer rendering

## Tests
- Pure ASCII returns correct width
- Mixed ASCII/Unicode returns None from `ascii_width`
- Empty string returns Some(0)
- Integration tests: wrapping/truncation correctness unchanged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ascii_width` and `display_width` exist.
- [ ] ASCII path is allocation-free.
- [ ] Non-ASCII path matches `unicode_width`.
- [ ] Integrated into the ftui-text measurement code paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:52:10.389324927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790245126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790179993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-v857w","title":"Create consolidated Getting Started guide for library consumers","description":"Issue #10: Consumer documentation exists but is scattered and has API drift. The agent-harness.md tutorial (440 lines) is comprehensive but uses outdated API patterns (App::inline() instead of actual App::new().screen_mode()). README is 1600+ lines, burying the getting-started path. New users cannot find a clear adoption path.

APPROACH: Fix existing docs first, then create minimal landing page. Do NOT duplicate tutorial content.

Implementation:

Phase 1 - Fix API Drift in Existing Docs:
1. Audit docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md code examples against actual API:
   - Verify App::inline(), App::fullscreen() calls match actual constructor patterns
   - Verify import paths match current crate structure
   - Fix any examples that use deprecated Program::new() (README lines 92-138)
   - Ensure all code blocks compile against current crates
2. Update README "Minimal API Example" section (lines 92-138) to use current API
3. Verify all 5 examples in crates/ftui-harness/examples/ compile and run:
   - minimal.rs (77 lines), streaming.rs (162 lines), counter.rs (95 lines), layout.rs, modal.rs

Phase 2 - Add CI Gate for Doc Examples:
4. Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml docs job:
   - name: Test doc examples
     run: cargo test --doc --workspace
   This catches future API drift in rustdoc examples automatically.

5. Add example compilation check:
   - name: Build examples
     run: cargo build --examples -p ftui-harness
   Ensures all 5 examples compile on every CI run.

Phase 3 - Create Minimal Getting Started Landing Page:
6. Create docs/getting-started.md (target: < 200 lines, NOT a tutorial):
   - "Start Here" header with 1-paragraph project summary
   - Dependency setup: which crates to add to Cargo.toml
     - Published on crates.io: ftui-core, ftui-layout, ftui-i18n
     - Not yet published (use git dep): ftui-harness, ftui-widgets, ftui-extras, etc.
   - Decision tree:
     - "I want an inline CLI UI" -> minimal.rs example + agent-harness.md Part 1
     - "I want full-screen TUI" -> agent-harness.md Part 5 (Inline vs Alt-Screen)
     - "I want to capture subprocess output" -> agent-harness.md Part 6 (PTY)
   - Examples index table:
     | Example | Use Case | Size | Run Command |
     |---------|----------|------|-------------|
     | minimal.rs | Inline UI with log viewer | 77 LOC | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example minimal |
     | streaming.rs | High-volume log output | 162 LOC | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example streaming |
     | counter.rs | State management | 95 LOC | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example counter |
     | layout.rs | Flex/grid composition | - | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example layout |
     | modal.rs | Modal dialogs | - | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example modal |
   - WIP status: clearly state API stability expectations
   - Link to agent-harness.md tutorial for hands-on walkthrough
   - Link to docs/concepts/screen-modes.md for architecture understanding

7. Add prominent link in README.md after TL;DR section (around line 20):
   > **New here?** Start with [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md) for dependency setup and a guided path to your first FrankenTUI app.

TESTS:

Doc Tests (CI-enforced):
- cargo test --doc --workspace: all rustdoc examples compile and pass
- cargo build --examples -p ftui-harness: all 5 examples compile
- cargo run -p ftui-harness --example minimal -- --help: minimal example runs without panic

Content Validation (manual checklist):
- [ ] All code blocks in agent-harness.md match actual API
- [ ] All code blocks in getting-started.md match actual API
- [ ] README minimal example compiles
- [ ] Decision tree links resolve to correct docs
- [ ] Published crate versions match Cargo.toml

Link Validation:
- [ ] All relative links in getting-started.md resolve
- [ ] README -> getting-started.md link works
- [ ] getting-started.md -> agent-harness.md link works
- [ ] getting-started.md -> examples link works

 {"id":"bd-vins","title":"Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes","description":"## Background
The typewriter effect is limited to left-to-right reveal. A comprehensive reveal system enables dramatic text appearances for titles, announcements, and onboarding.

## Implementation

Add RevealMode enum (used as parameter, not nested in TextEffect):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum RevealMode {
    #[default]
    LeftToRight,    // Classic typewriter
    RightToLeft,    // Reverse typewriter  
    CenterOut,      // Expand from middle
    EdgesIn,        // Converge to center
    Random,         // Random order (deterministic with seed)
    ByWord,         // Word at a time
    ByLine,         // Line at a time (for multi-line)
}
```

Add TextEffect variant (consistent structure with others):
```rust
TextEffect::Reveal {
    mode: RevealMode,
    progress: f64,      // 0.0 = hidden, 1.0 = fully revealed
    seed: u64,          // For Random mode determinism
}

// Separate variant for gradient mask (different parameters)
TextEffect::RevealMask {
    angle: f64,         // Sweep angle in degrees (0 = left-to-right)
    progress: f64,      // Mask position 0.0-1.0
    softness: f64,      // Edge softness 0.0 (hard) to 1.0 (gradient)
}
```

### Visibility Calculation by Mode:

| Mode | Visible When |
|------|--------------|
| LeftToRight | char_idx < progress * total_chars |
| RightToLeft | char_idx >= (1-progress) * total_chars |
| CenterOut | distance_from_center < progress * max_distance |
| EdgesIn | distance_from_edge < progress * max_distance |
| Random | hash(seed, idx) < progress * total_chars |
| ByWord | word_idx < progress * total_words |
| ByLine | line_idx < progress * total_lines |
| RevealMask | rotated_position > threshold (with softness fade) |

### Usage:
```rust
// Simple left-to-right reveal
StyledText::new("Hello World")
    .effect(TextEffect::Reveal { 
        mode: RevealMode::LeftToRight, 
        progress: 0.5,
        seed: 0,
    })

// Dramatic gradient mask sweep
StyledText::new("WELCOME")
    .effect(TextEffect::RevealMask {
        angle: 45.0,
        progress: 0.5,
        softness: 0.3,
    })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reveal_ltr_first_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows first half
- test_reveal_rtl_last_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows last half
- test_reveal_center_out_middle_first: Center chars visible first
- test_reveal_edges_in_edges_first: Edge chars visible first
- test_reveal_random_deterministic: Same seed = same reveal order
- test_reveal_random_all_chars_reveal: progress=1 shows all
- test_reveal_by_word_whole_words: Partial progress shows complete words
- test_reveal_by_line_whole_lines: Lines reveal atomically
- test_reveal_mask_angle_0: Left-to-right sweep
- test_reveal_mask_angle_90: Top-to-bottom sweep  
- test_reveal_mask_softness_0: Hard edge (binary visible/hidden)
- test_reveal_mask_softness_1: Gradient fade across chars

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot CenterOut at progress 0.25, 0.5, 0.75
- Snapshot RevealMask at angle=45, softness=0.3

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-vmuax","title":"E2E: Doom/Quake braille visual effects (input + render hashes)","description":"Scope: E2E script for DoomE1M1 and QuakeE1M1 braille renderers. Navigate to Visual Effects, select Doom and Quake, simulate inputs (WASD, mouse look, fire), and capture per-frame hashes.

Logging: JSONL with action, effect, frame index, hash, dims, seed, FPS estimate, and input sequence markers.

Considerations: Deterministic inputs and seeds; verify both alt-screen and inline modes.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Script runs for Doom and Quake in alt + inline at 120x40 (and 80x24).
2) JSONL logs capture per-frame hashes and input events.
3) Hashes stable across runs with fixed input sequence.","notes":"Progress: added effect/mode/size env overrides to scripts/demo_visual_fx_e2e.sh; added tests/e2e/scripts/test_visual_fx_fps.sh wrapper (runs Doom/Quake only) and wired into tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh.

@@ -80,6 +80,39 @@ jobs:
         run: cargo test --workspace --all-features

+  # ==========================================================================
+  # Docs Job: Rustdoc + Examples
+  # ==========================================================================
+  docs:
+    name: Docs (rustdoc + examples)
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
+        with:
+          toolchain: nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry
+        uses: actions/cache@v4
+        with:
+          path: |
+            ~/.cargo/registry
+            ~/.cargo/git
+            target
+          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-docs-nightly-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
+          restore-keys: |
+            ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-docs-nightly-
+
+      - name: Run rustdoc tests
+        run: cargo test --doc --workspace
+
+      - name: Build ftui-harness examples
+        run: cargo build --examples -p ftui-harness
+
   # ==========================================================================
   # Feature Combinations Job

 Key concepts:
 - `Model` with `update()` and `view()`
-- `App::inline` for scrollback-preserving inline mode
+- `App::new(...).screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { .. })` for scrollback-preserving inline mode
 - `Event` messages via `From<Event> for Event` (built in)
 
 Layout cheat sheet (inline mode, UI pinned at bottom):
@@ -346,8 +344,8 @@ fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
 ## Part 5: Inline vs Alt-Screen
 
 Current runtime selection is fixed at startup:
-- Inline mode: `App::inline(model, ui_height)` or `.screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { .. })`
-- Alt-screen: `App::fullscreen(model)` or `.screen_mode(ScreenMode::AltScreen)`
+- Inline mode: `.screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: .. })` (or `App::inline(model, ui_height)`)
+- Alt-screen: `.screen_mode(ScreenMode::AltScreen)` (or `App::fullscreen(model)`)
 
 There is no runtime API for switching screen modes mid-session yet. If you need
 full-screen modal behavior today, spawn a separate fullscreen program or exit
